title: Hive MCP Registry Manifest
description: Schema for portable MCP server manifests with optional Hive-specific extensions.

^_comment(?:_[A-Za-z0-9-]+)?$.description: Human-readable note ignored by registry tooling.

$schema.description: Canonical URL for this manifest schema.

name.description: Portable registry identifier for the MCP server.

display_name.description: Human-friendly name shown in UIs and docs.

version.description: Semantic version of the MCP server manifest entry.

description.description: Short summary of what the MCP server does.

author.description: Original creator of the MCP server.

maintainer.description: Current maintainer responsible for registry updates.

repository.description: Source repository URL for the MCP server.

license.description: Open source license identifier or text label for the server.

status.description: Trust tier assigned by the registry maintainers.

docs_url.description: Primary documentation URL for installation and usage.

items.description: Operating system supported by this server.

supported_os.description: Operating systems the server is expected to support.

deprecated.description: Whether this manifest points to a deprecated server.

deprecated_by.description: Registry name of the preferred replacement server.

transport.description: Transport modes supported by this MCP server.

install.description: Install sources for common package ecosystems.

stdio.description: Configuration for launching the server over stdio.

http.description: Configuration for launching or reaching the server over HTTP.

unix.description: Configuration for launching or reaching the server over a Unix domain socket.

sse.description: Configuration for launching or reaching the server over Server-Sent Events.

tools.description: Tool declarations exposed by the MCP server.

credentials.description: Credentials and environment variables required by the server.

items.description: Discovery tag describing the server domain or ecosystem.

tags.description: Search tags used for catalog filtering.

items.description: High-level category label for the server.

categories.description: Broad product or workflow categories used by clients.

mcp_protocol_version.description: MCP protocol version implemented by the server.

commentField.description: Human-readable note ignored by registry tooling.

name.description: Display name of the original author.

github.description: GitHub username of the original author.

url.description: Primary homepage or profile URL for the original author.

github.description: GitHub username for the current maintainer.

email.description: Contact email for registry review follow-up.

items.description: One supported transport for this server.

supported.description: Transports that this server can speak.

pip.description: PyPI package name, or null when pip is not supported.

docker.description: Docker image reference, or null when Docker is not supported.

npm.description: npm package name, or null when npm is not supported.

name.description: Portable snake_case tool name exposed by the server.

description.description: Short explanation of what the tool does.

tool.description: One tool exposed by the MCP server.

id.description: Stable credential identifier used by clients.

env_var.description: Environment variable name used to supply the credential.

description.description: Explanation of what the credential is for.

help_url.description: URL explaining how to obtain or configure the credential.

required.description: Whether this credential is required for core functionality.

command.description: Executable used to launch the server in stdio mode.

items.description: One launch argument passed to the stdio command.

args.description: Arguments passed when launching the stdio server.

default_port.description: Default TCP port used by the HTTP server.

health_path.description: Relative health-check path exposed by the HTTP server.

command.description: Executable used to launch the server in HTTP mode.

items.description: One launch argument passed to the HTTP command.

args.description: Arguments passed when launching the HTTP server.

httpTransport.description: Launch and connection information for HTTP transport.

socket_template.description: Socket path template, optionally using {socket_path} or {name}.

command.description: Executable used to launch the server in Unix socket mode.

items.description: One launch argument passed to the Unix socket command.

args.description: Arguments passed when launching the Unix socket server.

unixTransport.description: Launch and connection information for Unix domain socket transport.

url_template.description: SSE endpoint template, optionally using {port} or {name}.

command.description: Executable used to launch the server in SSE mode.

items.description: One launch argument passed to the SSE command.

args.description: Arguments passed when launching the SSE server.

sseTransport.description: Launch and connection information for SSE transport.

min_version.description: Minimum Hive version that can consume this manifest.

max_version.description: Maximum Hive version supported, or null when open-ended.

items.description: One Hive profile where this server fits well.

profiles.description: Hive profile recommendations for this server.

tool_namespace.description: Preferred Hive namespace prefix for the server's tools.

example_agent.description: URL to an example Hive agent that uses this server.
